text: Concurrent strength and endurance training blunts lower-body strength adaptations in males but not in females, with a standardized mean difference of -0.43 in males versus 0.08 in females.
Concurrent training does not produce sex differences in upper-body strength adaptations.
Concurrent training does not produce sex differences in power adaptations.
Concurrent training does not produce sex differences in VO2max adaptations.
Untrained individuals show impaired VO2max gains from concurrent training compared to trained or highly trained endurance athletes.
Trained and highly trained endurance athletes do not show impaired VO2max adaptations from concurrent strength and endurance training.
population: trained and highly trained endurance athletes

text: Training status (untrained vs trained) does not affect strength or power interference from concurrent training.
